description: Dual-track forecast accuracy log. opening_bias = gap direction scored at morning open. session_direction = close direction scored at EOD. Both tracks are independent.

lessons: IT sector can decouple from geopolitical risk when USD strengthens + global tech rotates — add as factor M011_IT_USD_DECOUPLING
De-escalation narrative doesn't need to be confirmed for market to rally — markets anticipate before news
Should have weighted higher probability on 'wait and see' outcome (Session A actual = market waited for Apr 7 deadline, not Apr 6 deadline)
sessions.proposed_improvement: Add IT/USD correlation check to forecast: if USD strengthening AND global tech in rally, add +0.3-0.5% bullish buffer to Nifty forecast regardless of geopolitical regime

actual.session_theme: CEASEFIRE EUPHORIA + RBI RELIEF — Trump 2-week suspension confirmed at 5:30 AM IST, Iran accepted, Hormuz safe passage protocol. Nifty best single-day rally in 11 months (+873.70 pts). Brent crashed -17.55% to $93.13. RBI rate hold added further confidence. Top sectors: Aviation (+8-10%), Auto (+7-9%), Paints. IndiGo hit upper circuit limit at ₹4,614.90.

accuracy.direction_note: Forecast DOWN -0.8%, actual UP +3.78% — direction WRONG. Underestimated probability of DEAL scenario (assigned 20%, actual = 100%).
accuracy.magnitude_note: Forecast range 22,600-23,000 (DOWN). Actual 23,997.35 — 997 pts ABOVE forecast range. Magnitude error = -4.58% (actual +3.78% vs forecast -0.8%).
accuracy.brent_note: Forecast $108-115. Actual $93.13. Crude crashed 17.55% below forecast due to ceasefire + Hormuz reopening + demand destruction fears easing.

lessons: Geopolitical binary forecasts underweighted 'best case' outcomes. When Trump sets a deadline with 3 possible outcomes, markets price in probability-weighted view. This forecast weighted (40% extension, 40% strikes, 20% deal) but market knew deal was Trump's preference = should have weighted deal higher.
Ceasefire announcement = regime shift from ESCALATION to DE-ESCALATION is BINARY. NOT incremental. April 8 proved this — a single diplomatic breakthrough overrode 3 days of fear premium.
Commodities are leading — when crude crashes 17% in one session, equity indices rally on cost relief. Should monitor crude for early regime signals.
RBI rate decisions can amplify geopolitical relief rallies — always check RBI/central bank calendar when forecasting on binary event days.
sessions.error_root_cause: FORECAST_PAT003_GEOPOLITICAL_BINARY_UNDERWEIGHTING — when a deadline has 3+ possible outcomes with asymmetric payoffs, markets reveal a revealed preference for the best outcome sooner than consensus expects. April 8 ceasefire proved this.
sessions.proposed_improvement: For binary geopolitical forecasts with 3+ outcomes: Run scenario-weighted forecast (40% A outcome, 30% B outcome, 20% C outcome) but ALSO mark the 'best case' outcome as a reversal trigger. April 8: best case WAS deal; when deal materialized, all bearish assumptions collapsed. Next time, flag 'if deal → regime flips to +2-4% Nifty rally'. Also check RBI calendar for same-day policy decisions.

forecast.key_assumption: Post-ceasefire profit-booking. TCS Q4 results (April 9). April 10 Islamabad talks. FII flows uncertain.

actual.session_theme: POST-CEASEFIRE PULLBACK. Trump stated US-Iran situation 'remains tense until a real pact is reached' — dampening optimism. Bloomberg: 'India Rally Shows Signs of Short Covering, Lacks Fresh Buying'. TCS Q4FY26 announced post-market (strong beat — revenue ₹70,698 cr, TCV $12B record, dividend ₹31/share) but Nifty had already closed by then.

accuracy.direction_note: Forecast FLAT_TO_SLIGHTLY_UP, actual DOWN -0.93%. Direction WRONG.
accuracy.magnitude_note: Forecast range 23,800-24,100. Actual close 23,775.10 — 25 pts BELOW lower bound. Near-miss but outside range.
accuracy.brent_note: Brent estimated stable ~$93-95. Within forecast $90-97 range. CORRECT.

lessons: Post-ceasefire sessions are not automatic 'hold gains' sessions — headline risk from the same actor (Trump) can re-introduce doubt same-day
Bloomberg 'short covering, lacks fresh buying' signal — when a 3.78% rally is driven by short covering rather than fresh institutional longs, the next session is vulnerable to reversal
Positive post-market results (TCS) can't prevent intraday selling when the regime signal is mixed — the market prices the live headline, not the after-hours good news
April 9 miss was a very near-miss (25 pts below range) — model calibration is acceptable, but the direction call needs to incorporate 'Trump headline risk' on Iran even during ceasefire days
sessions.error_root_cause: TRUMP_CEASEFIRE_AMBIGUITY — Trump's comment 'remains tense until real pact' on ceasefire Day 2 introduced doubt about the durability of the April 8 rally. Combined with Bloomberg's short-covering warning, market gave back gains without fresh catalysts. TCS results positive but post-market. Magnitude error: -0.73% vs forecast (25 pts below lower bound — very near miss).

forecast.base_case_scenario: Talks Neutral (45%) — ceasefire holds, no breakthrough
forecast.key_assumption: Islamabad talks 10AM IST — VP Vance vs FM Araghchi. Nuclear enrichment red line vs Hormuz oversight demand. Talks likely neutral/ongoing. TCS gap-up provides IT floor.

actual.session_theme: TALKS_NEUTRAL — Islamabad talks concluded without breakthrough or collapse. Ceasefire holds through April 22. TCS sell-on-news confirmed (₹2,539.80). Brent intraday low $92 on Trump delay announcement before recovering to $95.95. Nifty closed inside P016 range.

accuracy.direction_note: Forecast BINARY_VOLATILE — base TALKS_NEUTRAL. Actual: talks neutral, Nifty +1.16%. Direction correct (market moved up on neutral talks outcome vs TALKS_FAIL fear).
accuracy.magnitude_note: Nifty closed at ~24,050 — inside P016 range 23,900–24,100 ✓. Forecast range partially vindicated.
accuracy.brent_note: Brent at $95.95 — slightly above the $90-97 de-escalation zone forecast. Intraday touched $92 on Trump delay news. Close call.

lessons: TALKS_NEUTRAL outcome is mildly bullish — markets price in 'disaster averted' even without progress. April 10 proved this: no breakthrough, yet Nifty +1.16%.
TCS sell-on-news confirmed: stock dropped despite Q4 beat. IT earnings catalyst timing matters as much as the beat itself.
Brent intraday volatility on geopolitical headlines can be large (-4% intraday) but mean-reverts within the session. End-of-day price is more signal than intraday swings.

session_direction.key_assumption: US Hormuz blockade starts today + Brent >$100 = sustained war premium, no intraday relief rally without ceasefire news

session_direction_accuracy.direction_note: Forecast DOWN, actual DOWN. Direction CORRECT but magnitude underestimated. Forecast -2.5%, actual -0.91% — market held better than expected.
session_direction_accuracy.range_note: Nifty closed at 23,830 — INSIDE range 23300-23650. Within forecast envelope.
session_direction_accuracy.brent_note: Forecast: Brent >$100. Actual $102.26. Within expectation but spike faster than base model ($101-103 range expected, actual $102). Close call.

actual.session_theme: US-IRAN TALKS FAIL + HORMUZ BLOCKADE — Ceasefire collapse over weekend (April 11) triggered war premium resumption. US naval blockade of Iranian ports announced April 13 10 AM ET. Brent spiked to $102.26 (+6.58%). Nifty opened -1.92% gap down at 23,589, recovered intraday to 23,830 (-0.91% from prev close). FII selling continues. Oil & Gas sector down 3%. Aviation/Airlines weak. Defensive sectors (Gold, Pharma) tracking positively but broad selloff prevented rally. War premium fully priced in at Brent $102+.

lessons: Geopolitical talk failures are more impactful on intraday than sustained trend reversals. April 13 proved: Nifty gapped down hard (-1.92%) but recovered +240 pts intraday. This is classic 'fear spike + fade' when the underlying regime (blockade escalation) is already fully priced.

Defense stocks as war premium indicator: ASTRA_MICRO at 1,132 (P012 target exceeded). When war premium is sustainable (not temporary talks failure), defense names hold and extend. April 13 confirmed P012 RIGHT — defense premium durable through blockade escalation.

Opening bias models need refinement for Talk-Failure days. GIFT Nifty -1.25% gap forecast was good, but actual -1.92% gap shows markets price Iran IRGC threats more severely at Asia open than US models predict. Incorporate IRGC_THREAT_SEVERITY_INDEX for future geopolitical talk failures.

opening_bias_accuracy.direction_note: Forecast GAP_DOWN -1.25%, actual GAP_DOWN -1.92%. Direction correct but magnitude underestimated by 0.67%.

sessions.holiday_name: Ambedkar Jayanti (Dr. B.R. Ambedkar Birth Anniversary)
lessons: April 14 holiday: Iran reached out to Trump, wants negotiations — Brent fell from $102.26 to $97.76 (-1.61%). War premium declining but not resolved.
Blockade still active. April 22 deadline = 8 days. Diplomatic channel opened changes probability distribution for next week.
Defense stocks like ASTRA MICRO at risk of further profit-booking if Iran-US formal talks are confirmed.

key_global_events.iran_us: Iran reached out to Trump, wanting negotiations. No formal deal yet. Blockade still in effect.

key_drivers: Brent $102.26 baseline — war premium sustained
US naval blockade of Iranian ports ACTIVE (Day 3)
April 14 = market holiday (Ambedkar Jayanti) — no domestic session to buffer
S&P 500 futures flat overnight (April 13 US session +1% — 'Iran war losses wiped out')
Iran IRGC 'last warning' still active — no formal ceasefire
opening_bias.note: Generated by EOD wrap April 13. Based on Brent $102.26 and blockade escalation sustained.

opening_bias_actual.scoring_note: Forecast GAP_DOWN -0.80% (Gift Nifty 23,641). Actual GIFT Nifty pre-market opened at 24,054 on April 15 at 1:45 AM IST (confirmed via live data). Estimated NSE open ~24,100 (+1.08% gap up). Direction WRONG — actual GAP_UP vs forecast GAP_DOWN. Key reason: Trump's April 14 statement that Iran-US talks 'could happen in next 2 days' drove overnight risk-on in GIFT Nifty. Brent declining from $102.26 to ~$95 range added further positive momentum. Prior EOD April 14 estimate of nifty_open=23,772 (GAP_DOWN) was incorrect — did not account for GIFT Nifty surge on US risk-on sentiment.
opening_bias_actual.gift_source: 5paisa.com GIFT Nifty live data (1:45 AM IST April 15)
opening_bias_actual.correction_note: Prior estimate nifty_open=23,772 (GAP_DOWN -0.25%) corrected to GAP_UP. GIFT Nifty opened at 24,054 confirming strong positive open.

session_direction.key_assumption: Brent eased to $97.50 on Iran outreach signal (Trump says Iran called, wants deal). No formal ceasefire yet — blockade still in effect. April 22 deadline 7 days away. Brent decline reduces inflation drag on financials/autos. Iran outreach creates diplomatic optionality but not resolution.

escalation.description: Iran rejects outreach / IRGC incident in Hormuz — blockade hardens

session_direction.brent_forecast: $95-100 (declining from $102.26 — Iran outreach reducing war premium)

Trump April 22 deadline — markets may still price uncertainty
FII flows — sustained selling can cap any rally even on positive Iran news
key_opportunities: MRPL / Refiners: Brent at $97.50 vs $102.26 = GRM recovery thesis back on track
IndiGo / Airlines: ATF cost relief returns if Brent holds below $100
Banking/Financials: Bounce potential as rate concern eases with lower crude
SETFGOLD: Monitor — war premium easing slightly, but still above $95 = hold

session_direction_accuracy.direction_note: Forecast FLAT_TO_SIDEWAYS_UP (+0.3%), actual STRONG_UP (+1.63%). Direction correct (positive), but magnitude significantly underestimated. The 30% probability relief rally scenario (24,000-24,200) materialized and exceeded even that range (close 24,231). Primary base case was too conservative at FLAT_TO_SIDEWAYS.
session_direction_accuracy.range_note: Nifty closed at 24,231.30. Forecast range was 23,700-24,100. Close was 131 pts ABOVE upper bound. Even the relief rally scenario (24,000-24,200) underestimated by 31 pts. Session exceeded all modeled upside scenarios.
session_direction_accuracy.brent_note: Brent forecast $95-100. Actual $96.70. Within forecast band.
session_direction_accuracy.opening_bias_note: Forecast GAP_DOWN -0.8%, actual GAP_UP +1.08% (scored in morning brief)

actual.session_theme: STRONG RELIEF RALLY — Iran-US second round talks imminent (Trump: within 2 days). Brent $96.70 (+2.01% on WTI supply concerns but war premium declining). Nifty opened 24,100 GAP_UP +1.08%, rallied through session to close 24,231 (+1.63%). All sectors positive. Aviation (+4.76%), Defense/Infra/PSU strong. SUZLON +7.13%. Session FAR EXCEEDED FLAT_TO_SIDEWAYS_UP forecast range (23,700-24,100).

lessons: When Trump explicitly signals talks "within 2 days" + Brent already declining, assign 50%+ probability to the relief rally scenario, not 30%

If opening bias forecast is WRONG (GAP_DOWN forecast but GAP_UP actual by >1%), immediately revise session direction upward — strong opens signal institutional conviction that overrides individual analyst conservatism
BLOCKADE_ESCALATION_STABILIZED regime + diplomatic optionality = markets run ahead of fundamentals; Nifty can trade 24,200+ even with Brent $96+ if diplomatic narrative is positive

key_drivers: Nifty closed +1.63% at 24,231 — profit booking likely at open
Brent $96.70 (+2.01%) — slightly elevated, adds caution
Iran-US second round talks "within 2 days" from April 14 — could be today (April 16)
IREDA Q4 earnings: FY26 loan book ₹93,075 Cr (+22% YoY) — potential PSU catalyst
April 22 ceasefire expiry = 6 days — urgency factor
opening_bias.note: Final EOD April 15 forecast. Revised from preliminary FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_GAP_UP 23,890 to FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_GAP_DOWN 24,170 given actual April 15 close of 24,231. Profit booking after strong +1.63% session expected at open.

sessions.opening_bias_rationale: Brent eased to $97.76 on Iran outreach. GIFT Nifty expected ~23,900-24,000. No overnight negative catalyst. Iran-US dialogue optionality positive. April 22 deadline still 8 days away — not yet panic-inducing.

opening_bias_actual.scoring_note: Forecast FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_GAP_DOWN -0.25% (GIFT Nifty ~24,170). Actual NSE open 24,150 (-0.34% gap down). Direction CORRECT. Magnitude within tolerance (-0.25% forecast vs -0.34% actual = 0.09% error). First fully CORRECT opening bias call. Brent declined from $96.70 to ~$94.89 overnight supporting mild gap-down thesis. Profit booking after +1.63% session materialized as expected.

session_direction.key_assumption: Iran-US second round talks being arranged (Pakistan army chief in Tehran). Brent declining to $94.66 reduces war premium. Ceasefire extension discussions positive. Market already rallied from 24,150 open to 24,304 by 10 AM — strong intraday buying. France-UK Hormuz maritime conference Friday adds diplomatic momentum. April 22 ceasefire expiry 6 days away creates urgency for deal.

talks_progress.description: Second round talks confirmed for April 16-17. Brent falls toward $92-93. Rally extends.

base_case_consolidation.description: Talks expected but not yet formally confirmed. Brent holds $93-96. Consolidation above 24,200.

disappointment_or_incident.description: IRGC autonomous action or talks breakdown. Brent spikes >$98. Sharp reversal.

sessions.session_direction_rationale: IREDA earnings key catalyst. Brent declining trend continues. Iran outreach = base case of TALKS_NEUTRAL (50%) or TALKS_PROGRESS (20%). No major negative catalyst unless IRGC incident.

session_direction_accuracy.direction_note: Forecast BULLISH +0.5%, actual DOWN -0.14%. Direction WRONG — market was flat-to-slightly-negative despite diplomatic momentum. Session opened strong (gap-up) but selling pressure through the day erased gains. Profit-booking and Friday caution ahead of April 22 deadline weighed.
session_direction_accuracy.range_note: Nifty closed at 24196.75 — INSIDE forecast range 24,150-24,500. Range calibration was good despite direction miss.
session_direction_accuracy.brent_note: Brent forecast $93-96. Actual $95.85. Within forecast band. CORRECT.

lessons: BULLISH forecasts during DIPLOMACY_ACCELERATING regime need a risk-off discount when heavyweight banks are distributing — HDFC Bank -1.78% dragged Nifty despite broad market being positive
Iran counter-threats (blocking Gulf/Red Sea shipping) can offset diplomatic optimism within the same session — incorporate dual-signal processing for geopolitical sessions
Friday sessions before geopolitical weekends (April 22 deadline 5 days away) see natural risk-off positioning — add Friday discount factor to bullish forecasts near binary event deadlines

actual.session_theme: FLAT SESSION WITH INTRADAY REVERSAL — Opened with gap-up optimism on Iran-US ceasefire extension talks (Trump: war "very close to over"). Market rallied early but reversed through session as (1) profit-booking set in after 2 consecutive up sessions, (2) Iran threatened to block Persian Gulf/Red Sea shipping if US blockade continues, (3) Friday caution ahead of April 22 ceasefire expiry. BankNifty -0.38% dragged indices. Broad market breadth positive (Adani Power +5.32%, IGIL +5.08%, Astra Micro +4.09%) but heavyweight selling (HDFC Bank -1.78%, Bharti Airtel -0.81%, Titan -1.34%) capped upside.

key_drivers: Nifty closed flat at 24196.75 after intraday reversal — momentum fading
Brent $95.85 slightly elevated (+0.97%) — Iran threatening Red Sea/Gulf shipping blockade
Friday session — natural risk-off ahead of weekend with April 22 ceasefire expiry 4 days away
Ceasefire extension talks ongoing but NOT formally agreed — uncertainty persists
France-UK Hormuz maritime conference Friday adds diplomatic variable
Trump optimism ("war very close to over") provides floor but Iran counter-threats cap upside

opening_bias_actual.scoring_note: Morning brief ran pre-market (02:43 IST). Opening data sourced from news reports (5paisa: GIFT Nifty -0.69% overnight; multiple outlets: Nifty near 24,200 in early trade). Forecast FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_GAP_DOWN -0.20%, actual ~-0.193% — direction correct, magnitude almost exact. EOD should validate with actual NSE open price.

session_direction.key_assumption: Weak open (GIFT -0.69%) bought aggressively. RELIANCE +1.58%, HDFC +0.60% recovering, SUZLON +4.34%, IRCON +8.5%, IREDA +3.3%, MRPL +1.81% — strong defense/infra/renewables + heavyweight bid. Partially offset by IT/private bank softness (ICICI -0.55%, INFY -0.43%, LT -0.42%, TCS flat). Ceasefire extension still under Bloomberg-reported discussion (not yet formal). Brent ~$95-98 holding. Friday ahead of April 22 ceasefire deadline. Dip-buying thesis prevails unless Iran escalation signal emerges.
session_direction.brent_forecast: $95-98 (elevated on Iran counter-threats, capped by extension talks)

morning_bid_sustains.description: Strong morning breadth (Reliance/defense/renewables) holds. No negative headline. Ceasefire extension chatter keeps bid in. Nifty closes modestly higher.

friday_profit_booking.description: Morning gains fade on Friday profit-booking ahead of April 22 weekend. Range-bound close near flat.

escalation_signal.description: Iran action on Gulf/Red Sea shipping, or ceasefire extension fails. Brent spikes >$100. Sharp reversal and selloff.

session_direction_accuracy.direction_note: Forecast FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_BULLISH +0.25%, actual +0.65% (strong bullish). Direction CORRECT. Magnitude underestimated by ~0.4% — Brent dropped -3.19% to $96.22 (war premium rapidly unwinding) stronger than expected. The morning_bid_sustains 50% scenario (+0.2% to +0.6%, 24,240-24,340) materialized at the upper bound.
session_direction_accuracy.range_note: Nifty closed 24,353.55 vs forecast range 24,100-24,350. Close is 3.55 pts above upper bound (0.015% over) — effectively in range within calibration tolerance. BankNifty +0.85% led indices (HDFC Bank +0.56% recovered from -1.78% prior day).
session_direction_accuracy.brent_note: Forecast $95-98. Actual $96.22 — INSIDE forecast band. War premium unwinding on ceasefire-extension progress and Trump Iran deal optimism.
session_direction_accuracy.opening_bias_note: Morning brief forecast FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_GAP_DOWN -0.20%, actual NSE open ~24,150 (-0.193%). Direction CORRECT. Magnitude almost exact.

actual.session_theme: STRONG FRIDAY RALLY ON BRENT COLLAPSE + CEASEFIRE EXTENSION PROGRESS — Nifty +0.65% to 24,353.55 (above key 24,400 resistance briefly). Brent -3.19% to $96.22 as US-Iran officials report "in principle agreement" to extend ceasefire before Tuesday April 21 expiry. Second round of talks expected. DMART +4.52%, IRCON +8.65%, SUZLON +5.33%, BRITANNIA +2.68%, PREMEXPLN +1.34% lead. Defense/infra/renewables broad-based rally. Dip-buy thesis fully played out — weak GIFT open (-0.69%) aggressively bought; session rallied through the day. IT lagged (WIPRO -2.83% on earnings) but didn't drag. Weekend risk: ceasefire expiry Tuesday — 1 trading day away on Monday.

lessons: DIPLOMATIC_MOMENTUM_AMPLIFIES_DIP_BUY — When Brent falls >3% intraday on ceasefire-extension headlines, dip-buy thesis can deliver 2x forecast magnitude. Friday profit-booking scenario (30% prob) did NOT materialize because Brent collapse was too strong.

RANGE_UPPER_BOUND_BREACH_IS_SCENARIO_MISSING — Only 3 scenarios modeled (bid_sustains / profit_booking / escalation). Missed a 4th: DIPLOMATIC_BREAKTHROUGH_SURGE which would extend beyond 24,340. Add scenario when ceasefire-extension talks have "in principle agreement" reported.

BRENT_FORECAST_CORRECT_BUT_CONSERVATIVE — Forecast $95-98. Actual $96.22 arrived via a sharp -3.19% move rather than stable drift. Volatile path within correct band = correct call, but magnitude of Brent move underwrote the Nifty magnitude miss.

key_drivers: Nifty closed +0.65% at 24,353.55 — strong close supports positive momentum into Monday
Brent $96.22 (-3.19%) — war premium collapsing on ceasefire extension "in principle agreement"
US-Iran second round talks expected near-term (Trump: war close to over)
April 21 (Tuesday) ceasefire expiry = 1 trading day away — positions caution Monday afternoon
Weekend event risk: Iran IRGC Hormuz incident or formal ceasefire extension announcement
Friday strong breadth (DMART +4.52%, IRCON +8.65%, SUZLON +5.33%) — momentum carryover likely

opening_bias_actual.scoring_note: Logged forecast (eod_wrap_2026-04-17): FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_GAP_UP +0.15% (GIFT est 24,390). Actual: Nifty opened 24,338.15 (-0.063% gap-down from 24,353.55). GIFT Nifty preopen was ~24,449 (would have suggested gap-up +0.39%). Direction tilt was wrong (predicted up, got down) but magnitude within FLAT band (|actual|<0.1%) — PARTIAL credit. NOTE: a mid-cycle weekly_review_2026-04-19 update flipped the forecast to FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_GAP_DOWN -0.25% after weekend Trump-violation / Hormuz re-close news; that revised view had the correct direction but also overstated magnitude (-0.25% vs actual -0.063%).
opening_bias_actual.data_provenance: News reports 2026-04-20 AM (Republic World, News24, Open Magazine) — GIFT Nifty 24,449, Nifty open 24,338.15, Sensex open 78,442.18.

session_direction.key_assumption: Monday is pre-ceasefire-expiry positioning day. Early session has already shown high intraday vol (Nifty swung 24,241 -> 24,420, ~180pt range within first hour). Constructive factors: Friday momentum carryover (DMART, IRCON, SUZLON), financial heavyweights supportive (NIFTY Financial Services +0.67%), GIFT Nifty 24,449 suggested bulls were positioned. Negative factors: Brent rebound to $97-98 on Hormuz re-close, Trump "total violation" accusation, Kushner/Witkoff Islamabad talks still in progress (outcome unknown intraday). Expect choppy range with modest upside bias as market pre-prices ceasefire extension (55% probability per binary matrix), but hedging into Tuesday expiry will cap upside. HCLTECH results Tuesday PM add event-risk overlay.

session_direction.key_assumption: Market is aggressively pre-pricing ceasefire extension on Brent -5.49% overnight to $90.24 (finally gaining the diplomacy signal markets had sought since Islamabad restart). Nifty already +0.69% at 13:47 IST with BankNifty +1.33% and defense also rallying (ASTRA +3.95%, MAZDOCK +1.67%) — a broad risk-on move. Binary outcome still hinges on Islamabad talks + ceasefire expiry tonight (IST Wed). HCLTECH post-market results add event risk but M013 caps IT confidence. Base case: continued upward drift into close as positioning flows dominate; cap to 24,680 reflects expected pre-close hedging into dual binary (ceasefire+earnings).

session_direction.key_assumption: Ceasefire talks have collapsed: Iran refused to attend Islamabad talks, JD Vance cancelled Pakistan trip, US put Iran negotiations on hold. Brent crude surging to $97-102 (with spikes above $100). India markets open with a gap-down of ~0.5-0.6% and are likely to remain under sustained pressure. Defense stocks (ASTRA MICRO, MAZDOCK, COCHINSHIP) will surge on geopolitical premium returning. Broader market, financials, aviation, OMCs will face selling. This is a regime-change session back toward GEOPOLITICAL_RISK_ELEVATED.

session_direction_accuracy.direction_note: Forecast BEARISH_VOLATILE (-0.8%, range 23,900-24,350). Actual: Nifty closed 24,378.1 (-0.81%). Direction CORRECT. Magnitude nearly exact. Close was 28 pts ABOVE range top (24,350) — slightly milder bear session than forecast because Brent fell to $94.86, below the $95 floor the model assumed. 'Partial collapse uncertainty' scenario (40% prob) materialized with less crude pressure than expected.
session_direction_accuracy.range_note: Nifty closed at 24,378.1 vs forecast range 23,900-24,350. Missed range top by 28 pts. Brent at $94.86 (below $95 forecast floor) cushioned downside and kept Nifty above range. Very close miss.
session_direction_accuracy.brent_note: Brent forecast $95-108 (floor $95 only if talks revive). Actual: $94.86 — BELOW forecast floor. Brent fell -3.68% (prev $98.48) as some Hormuz transit resumed or diplomatic back-channels signaled easing. This was the key surprise that kept Nifty above 24,350.
session_direction_accuracy.opening_bias_note: No EOD wrap April 21 — no formal opening bias for April 22. Graded UNSCORED.

actual.session_theme: Ceasefire collapse confirmed (Iran refused talks, Vance cancelled), but Brent fell -3.68% to $94.86 (below model floor). Defense outperformed. Nifty -0.81%, BankNifty -0.43%. TECHM -2.55% on Q4 results (M013 confirmed). IREDA +8.99%, PREMEXPLN +7.71%, URBANCO +6.24% led gainers. Market absorbed geopolitical shock better than worst case.

lessons: Brent surprise: model set $95 as floor, actual was $94.86 — Brent can cool even in ceasefire collapse if Hormuz transit partially resumes. Add Hormuz partial-transit scenario to crude model.
Range calibration: forecast 23,900-24,350 but Nifty closed at 24,378 (+28 pts above). Tighter asymmetric range would have been better — model overestimated downside in 'partial collapse' scenario.
M013 validated again: TechM -2.55% on Q4 results day despite mixed results. SELL_ON_EARNINGS_NEWS_IT pattern holds.
Defense P025 scored ABOVE_RANGE (1129.75 vs target 1050-1100): ceasefire collapse accelerated defense run beyond target. Non-linear geopolitical premium harder to cap at a target range.

key_drivers: Brent cooled to $94.86 (-3.68%) — relative positive vs prior $98+ but still elevated vs <$90 comfort zone
Infosys Q4FY26 results day — M013 applies, IT sector sentiment sets tone
Ceasefire still technically in collapse phase — overnight geopolitical headlines could spike
HCLTECH Q4 results post-market Apr 22 — results out overnight will influence gap

opening_bias_actual.scoring_note: Forecast FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_GAP_DOWN (-0.2%). Actual gap +0.055% (24391.5 vs prev close 24378.1). The FLAT portion of the forecast was CORRECT — magnitude is tiny (<0.1%). The SLIGHT_GAP_DOWN directional bias was WRONG — market opened slightly positive. Overnight: Trump extended ceasefire (positive) but Iran seized 2 ships in Hormuz (negative) — net tiny positive. Brent re-spiked +3.14% to $101.57, negating some tailwind. Graded PARTIAL: regime (flat) correct, directional tilt flipped, magnitude within noise band.

session_direction.key_assumption: Infosys Q4FY26 results post-market on April 22 and intraday on April 23 will dominate IT sector. M013 (IT Regime Risk Filter) applies — do NOT expect IT breakout regardless of earnings quality. Brent cooling to $94.86 is constructive but needs 3+ days below $90 for regime relief. Ceasefire status remains binary overnight — any fresh escalation spikes Brent and triggers defense rotation. Defense stocks (ASTRA MICRO, MAZDOCK, COCHINSHIP) likely to consolidate after today's gains. BankNifty mildly bearish at -0.43% suggests credit risk premium still elevated. Session will be Infosys-led — watch for 'sell the print' vs 'beat and hold' dynamic in IT.

session_direction_accuracy.direction_note: Forecast VOLATILE_SIDEWAYS_SLIGHT_BEARISH (-0.2%). Actual -0.84% (-205.05 pts to 24,173.05). Direction correct (bearish), but magnitude ~4.2x stronger than forecast. HCLTECH post-market -10% gapped IT sector; Infosys Q4 beat (+20.87% YoY net profit) but FY27 guidance 1.5-3.5% CC in-line — triggered SELL_ON_EARNINGS_NEWS_IT (M013 confirmed again).
session_direction_accuracy.range_note: Nifty closed 24,173.05 — within forecast range 24,100-24,580 (73 pts above lower bound). Range forecast accurate. Intraday low 24,134.80 tested the support zone but held.
session_direction_accuracy.brent_note: Forecast Brent $92-98; actual close $97.76 (-4.07% intraday). At top of forecast range. Brent cooled sharply intraday despite overnight Hormuz ship seizure spike to $101.57 — suggests partial Hormuz de-escalation signals during the session.
session_direction_accuracy.magnitude_note: Magnitude -0.84% vs forecast -0.2% — 4.2x multiplier. BankNifty -1.43% signalled credit risk premium spike. Two-day cumulative selloff -1.65% from Apr 21 close.

actual.session_theme: Second consecutive bearish session (-0.84%). IT sector led the decline after HCLTECH Q4 guidance miss (post-market Apr 22, -10%) dragged into Infosys Q4 day. Infosys beat on profit (+20.87% YoY) but FY27 CC guidance of 1.5-3.5% was in-line (not a positive surprise) — classic sell-on-news. BankNifty -1.43% indicated broad risk-off in financials. Brent cooled -4.07% intraday as Hormuz de-escalation signals emerged, providing some late-session support but not enough to reverse the selloff. Silver -4.40% signals haven-trade partial unwinding.

lessons: M013 confirmed again (5th occurrence pattern-wise): IT sector sells the news regardless of earnings quality during active geopolitical regimes. Infosys +20.87% YoY profit beat but stock/IT sector underperformed. Conviction on this filter: VERY HIGH.

Magnitude calibration error in SLIGHT_BEARISH regime: when HCLTECH-type -10% result leads the open, the -0.2% SLIGHT forecast is systematically too conservative. Need to widen magnitude estimate to -0.5% to -1.0% when sector-leading result miss precedes the session.

Brent intraday can diverge sharply from overnight close even without new headlines. Brent -4.07% today despite no new diplomatic breakthrough — likely back-channel signals or commercial de-escalation. Lesson: do NOT anchor session risk view to overnight Brent; monitor intraday Brent prints against forecast band.

BankNifty -1.43% vs Nifty -0.84% suggests credit/financials sector is pricing broader risk-off than the index shows. When BankNifty underperforms Nifty by >50 bps in bearish sessions, escalation risk to next session is meaningfully higher.
Two-day cumulative -1.65% brings Nifty to -0.84% YTD band; technical oversold bounce probability increases for Friday (weekend positioning + RSI dip).

key_drivers: Brent cooled sharply intraday to $97.76 (-4.07%) — constructive signal after two-day selloff
Oversold technical conditions after cumulative -1.65% over Apr 22-23; Friday positioning typically sees short-covering

opening_bias_actual.scoring_note: Forecast FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_GAP_UP (+0.15%, GIFT est 24,210). Actual gap -0.24% (24,115.55 vs prev close 24,173.05) — GAP_DOWN direction opposite of forecast. Overnight regime change: Brent surged +7-8% from $97.76 close to $105-106 (Israel threatens Iran attacks, Hormuz shipping remains blocked, US-Iran ceasefire fraying). The constructive 'oversold bounce' thesis collapsed on Brent surge. FII selling extended to 4th session (~₹3,254 cr). Graded WRONG — direction opposite, magnitude off by 0.4pts. Missed signal: overnight Hormuz escalation risk materialized despite modeled 35% probability.

Key support 24,000 (psychological + recent swing); if breaks, 23,900 next. Upside capped at 24,200-24,220. Defense (ASTRA MICRO, MAZDOCK) likely to firm on geopolitical premium. OMC/Aviation (MRPL, INDIGO) under pressure from Brent surge. Mean-reversion bounce possible intraday but unlikely to hold into close.

eod_session_direction_for_audit.note: Preserved EOD forecast for audit — morning brief revised to BEARISH_VOLATILE after overnight Brent surge to $105-106 and geopolitical re-escalation.

patterns_observed.name: IT_USD_DECOUPLING
patterns_observed.description: When USD strengthens AND global tech stocks rally, Nifty tends to outperform bearish geopolitical forecasts by 0.5-1.0% due to IT sector weightage (~15% of Nifty)

patterns_observed.name: DEADLINE_EXTENSION_RELIEF
patterns_observed.description: When a geopolitical deadline is extended (not resolved), markets see brief relief rally on 'disaster averted for now' sentiment — even if underlying risk persists

patterns_observed.name: GEOPOLITICAL_BINARY_UNDERWEIGHTING
patterns_observed.description: When a binary geopolitical event has 3+ possible outcomes (strikes/extension/deal), markets price the 'best case' scenario sooner and with more probability than consensus forecasts assign. Binary resolution creates regime flips, not incremental moves.
patterns_observed.evidence: April 7 (IT decoupling anticipation before deadline) + April 8 (deal materialised at 100% probability vs 20% forecast weight). Both cases show markets price best-case outcomes faster than consensus.

framework_improvements_implemented.improvement: Added IT/USD correlation check to morning forecast generation. When USD strengthening AND global tech in rally mode, add +0.3-0.5% bullish buffer to Nifty forecast.
framework_improvements_implemented.triggered_by: Apr 7 session PARTIAL_CORRECT — missed IT sector decoupling

framework_improvements_implemented.improvement: For future binary geopolitical forecasts: Include REVERSAL TRIGGER conditions. When best-case outcome materializes (deal/ceasefire), regime flips to +2-4% Nifty rally, not +0.5% extension relief.
framework_improvements_implemented.triggered_by: Apr 8 session WRONG forecast — underweighted deal scenario impact on markets

framework_improvements_implemented.improvement: FORECAST_PAT003 (Geopolitical Binary Underweighting) PROMOTED to framework. Always apply +2-4% reversal trigger when best-case scenario in a 3-outcome binary materializes.
framework_improvements_implemented.triggered_by: 2 confirmed occurrences: April 7 (IT anticipation) + April 8 (ceasefire deal)

framework_improvements_implemented.improvement: Add RBI/central bank calendar check to forecast generation. RBI rate decisions on the same day as binary geopolitical events can amplify moves significantly.
framework_improvements_implemented.triggered_by: Apr 8 RBI rate unchanged decision amplified ceasefire rally
